key_insight: DSGNAR is a second-order training framework for PINNs that directly tackles the core problem of ill-conditioned optimization, enabling much higher accuracy and faster convergence than standard methods.
engineering_relevance: It makes PINNs more viable for practical PDE solving by delivering near-round-off solutions on challenging equations and scaling better across nonlinear, chaotic, multi-scale, and high-dimensional problems.
method: Uses a doubly-sketched Gauss-Newton approximation to make second-order optimization scalable.
Adds adaptive control of both regularization and step length for stable progress.
Targets improved conditioning of the PINN loss landscape rather than only tuning architecture or learning rates.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports gains across a benchmark suite, including relative L2 errors as low as 3×10^-16 in double precision, five orders of magnitude improvement on Burgers’ equation, and an eight-order improvement on a high-dimensional Poisson problem.
watch_item: The method depends on careful optimization and numerical precision choices, so performance may vary with problem setup, architecture, and floating-point limits.
paper_title: An Optimisation Framework for the Well-Conditioned Training of Physics-Informed Neural Networks
